headline: GATORADE ASSERTS THERE ARE NO SHORTCUTS TO GREATNESS IN NEW 'IS IT IN YOU?' GLOBAL FOOTBALL CAMPAIGN
summary: For decades, Gatorade has fueled football athletes at every level across the globe, from community pitches to the world's biggest stages. As Gatorade's world-class football roster – including Christian Pulisic, Vinícius Júnior, Julián Álvarez, Gilberto Mora, Luis Díaz and Tajon Buchanan – prepares to compete, the brand is launching a new global campaign, 'Trust What's Inside.' headline: Got 5,000? Coca-Cola vs. PepsiCo – One Has Quietly Outperformed for a Decade
summary: Coca-Cola (NYSE:KO) closed full-year 2025 with steady organic growth and a Zero Sugar brand accelerating. PepsiCo (NASDAQ:PEP) followed with a Q1 2026 beat showing international momentum and North America foods stabilizing. Willing to invest $5,000? Let’s see which has built the stronger decade-long case for investors. Zero Sugar Carries Coke. International Rescues Pepsi. Coca-Cola’s growth ... Got 5,000? headline: 5 Must-Read Analyst Questions From PepsiCo’s Q1 Earnings Call
summary: PepsiCo’s first quarter results were met with a positive market reaction, reflecting strong execution across core business areas. Management credited a combination of effective supply chain management, the benefits of prior productivity initiatives, and the accelerated rollout of new product innovations as key drivers of improved margins and revenue growth. CEO Ramon Laguarta highlighted the company’s ability to capture new consumption occasions and bring lapsed consumers back into the portfolio

raw.headline: 5 Must-Read Analyst Questions From PepsiCo’s Q1 Earnings Call

Management credited a combination of effective supply chain management, the benefits of prior productivity initiatives, and the accelerated rollout of new product innovations as key drivers of improved margins and revenue growth. CEO Ramon Laguarta highlighted the company’s ability to capture new consumption occasions and bring lapsed consumers back into the portfolio

headline: Sonoco Is Still a Buy After Earnings Selloff
summary: Investors in  Sonoco Products  which I presented to our Barron’s Investor Circle community last week, should probably settle down, as well.  Wednesday’s post-earnings selloff pushed the 127-year old packaging company’s stock down 16%, leaving some shareholders feeling like they are staring into an empty can of Pringles.  Additionally, as I highlighted to Barron’s Investor Circle readers, artificial intelligence is a surprising catalyst for the Hartsville, S.C.-based company.

raw.headline: Sonoco Is Still a Buy After Earnings Selloff

headline: Sonoco Selloff: Investors Should Can It! It’s a Buy.
summary: Investors in  Sonoco  Products, which I presented to our Barron’s Investor Circle community last Tuesday, should probably settle down, as well.  Wednesday’s post-earnings selloff pushed the 127-year old packaging company’s stock down 16%—leaving some shareholders feeling like they are staring into an empty can of Pringles.  In fact, on Wednesday’s conference call, CEO Howard Coker announced the opening of Sonoco’s new facility in Thailand with production capacity of over 200 million cans annually to supply the “growing stack chips market in Asia.”

raw.headline: Sonoco Selloff: Investors Should Can It! It’s a Buy.

Wednesday’s post-earnings selloff pushed the 127-year old packaging company’s stock down 16%—leaving some shareholders feeling like they are staring into an empty can of Pringles.  In fact, on Wednesday’s conference call, CEO Howard Coker announced the opening of Sonoco’s new facility in Thailand with production capacity of over 200 million cans annually to supply the “growing stack chips market in Asia.”

headline: Yum! Brands CEO on innovation & Pizza Hut's future
summary: Yum! Brands CEO, Chris Turner, chats with Yahoo Finance Executive Editor Brian Sozzi about his big plans for Taco Bell, what the future holds for KFC in the US, and why Pizza Hut may be sold soon.Plus, The Coffee Can Investor Author, Neeraj Khemlani, and portfolio manager, Matt Ankrum, talk about the future of investing and their outlook for the news business amid considerable industrywide cost-cutting.

headline: Alphabet Gets Street Backing As Cramer Doubles Down On $400 Target
summary: Alphabet shares gained 2.1% to settle at $339.32 on Wednesday; Arm shares gained 12% to settle at $196.57.
